name: membank

metadata.name: membank
metadata.summary: A library to handle persistent memory

documentation:
# membank
Python library for storing data in persistent memory (sqlite, postgresql, berkeley db)
## goals
Provide interface to database storage that automates heavy lifting of database setup, migration, table definition, query construction.
## quick intro
### add items to persistent storage
```python
import dataclasses as data # Python standard library

from membank import LoadMemory

@data.dataclass
class Dog():
    breed: str
    color: str = "black"
    weight: float = 0
    data: dict = data.field(default_factory=dict)
    picture: bytes = b''
    aliases: list = data.field(default_factory=list)

@data.dataclass
class DogWithID():
    id: str = data.field(default=None, metadata={"key": True})
    breed: str
    color: str = "black"
    weight: float = 0
    data: dict = data.field(default_factory=dict)
    picture: bytes = b''
    alive: bool = True

memory = LoadMemory() # defaults to sqlite memory
memory.put(Dog('Puli')) # stores object into database
dog = memory.get.dog() # retrieves first object found
assert dog.breed == 'Puli'
dog.color = "white"
memory.put(dog) # be carefull you store another dog
dog = memory.put(DogWithID("AB1234", "Puli"))
dog = memory.get.dogwithid(id="AB1234")
dog.color = "white"
memory.put(dog) # now you update existing dog

```
### retrieve those after
```python
memory = LoadMemory() # to make this work in new process, don't use sqlite memory
dog = memory.get.dog() # get first found or None
assert dog.color == 'black'
dogs = memory.get("dog") # always returns list (empty or with items)
assert len(dogs) >= 0
```
### editing returned objects
```python
dog = memory.get.dog()
dog.breed = 'Labdrador'
memory.put(dog) # stores edited object back
```
### filter objects
```python
dog = memory.get.dog(breed='Labdrador')
assert dog.breed == 'Labrador'
```
## Known issues
 - return class is stored(pickled) into underlying database table with reference to the module to load it back. If module name or class name is changed, membank won't always be able to load previous data as object pointer could be missing

documentation: All general errors in memory interface.

classes.name: membank.errors.MemoryTableDoesNotExist
documentation: Table does not exist in memory.

classes.name: membank.errors.MemoryFilteringError
documentation: Error with filtering different tables at the same time.

classes.name: membank.errors.MemoryOutOfSyncError
documentation: Error when memory is out of sync with database.

functions.name: membank.utils.assert_table_name
documentation:
Verify that instance is a dataclass instance.

Verify also that instance has all fields as per annotated types.
Return valid table name from instance.
Raise e.GeneralMemoryError otherwise.

modules.name: membank.datamapper
documentation: Module supports dataclass storage and retrieval.

classes.name: membank.datamapper.TableClass
documentation: Maps a dataclass to a Table.

classes.name: membank.datamapper.Mapper

methods.name: membank.datamapper.Mapper._handle_missing_meta
documentation:
Run function such that missing __meta_dataclasses are taken into account.

Upon exception that table __meta_dataclasses__ is missing, fix it and reraise,
otherwise just raises error.
Interface to store and retrieve dataclasses.

documentation: Defines interfaces exposed to library user

documentation: From Python data type py_type returns SQL type.

functions.name: membank.datamethods.filter_stmt
documentation: Prepare SQL statement and returns it.

functions.name: membank.datamethods.get_list
documentation: Get list of items from table.

documentation: Get all items from table as per SQL statement.

documentation: Create or update an item in table.

documentation: Sync table as per obj values.

functions.name: membank.datamethods.create_table
documentation:
Add a memory attribute.

Memory attribute must be instance of dataclass In database words
this adds a new Table

documentation: Unpack values from object using table as reference.

functions.name: membank.datamethods._get_selectables
documentation: Get column names in their respective order as per dataclass object.

Scan dataclass item for special metadata key value.

Return a dict of found item.

documentation: Check for valid path, raise GeneralError if any issue.

classes.name: membank.interface.MemoryBlob

documentation: Initialise blob with parent and getters.

methods.name: membank.interface.MemoryBlob.__call__
documentation:
Fetch result from memory.

Expects a table name or a table and a comparison as first argument.

Allows to access memory get method dynamically.

Exposes a get method that dynamically allows to access memory using
dataclass lowercased name:
- as a method
- as a first function argument

classes.name: membank.interface.LoadMemory

methods.name: membank.interface.LoadMemory.__init__
documentation:
Initialise memory with base settings.

debug - more verbose logging
url - resource locator according to RFC-1738 with scheme to designate database
type to be used, e.g. sqlite, postgresql, berkeleydb and scheme specific part
always follow either Common Internet Scheme Syntax or using File scheme part

Special note on relative vs. absolute file path handling
As RFC-1738 does not allow relative file paths, special notation is used only for
local file based access databases e.g. sqlite, berkeleydb. To make relative path,
host location of file path must be used i.e. file://{relative_path}. For absolute
paths host part must be empty i.e. file:///{abosulute_path}

methods.name: membank.interface.LoadMemory.clean_all_data
documentation: Remove all data and restores memory with all tables.
Loads memory and provides methods to create, change and access it.
